items.title: Samsung Biologics makes $1.8 billion all-cash offer for Switzerland's PolyPeptide Group
items.title_zh: Samsung Biologics makes $1.8 billion all-cash offer for Switzerland's PolyPeptide Group
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：South Korea's Samsung Biologics said on Monday that it had made an all-cash bid of 1.46 billion Swiss francs ($1.8 billion) for Switzerland's PolyPeptide Group, which specializes in peptide-based active pharmaceutical ingredients.；Under the terms of the public tender offer, stockholders will receive 44.31 Swiss franc per share.；The transaction is expected to close by the end of this year.
important_points: South Korea's Samsung Biologics said on Monday that it had made an all-cash bid of 1.46 billion Swiss francs ($1.8 billion) for Switzerland's PolyPeptide Group, which specializes in peptide-based active pharmaceutical ingredients.
Under the terms of the public tender offer, stockholders will receive 44.31 Swiss franc per share.
The transaction is expected to close by the end of this year.

items.title: World Cup 2026 final in photos: Spain triumphs as football crowns a new champion
items.title_zh: World Cup 2026 final in photos: Spain triumphs as football crowns a new champion
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：Spain reclaimed football's biggest prize on Sunday, defeating Argentina 1-0 after extra time in a tense World Cup final that delivered high drama both on and off the pitch.；Ferran Torres' decisive strike settled the contest, ending Argentina's title defense and handing Spain its second World Cup crown.；The defeat was especially poignant for Lionel Messi, who left the field in tears after collecting his runners-up medal in what is expected to be his final World Cup appearance.
important_points: Spain reclaimed football's biggest prize on Sunday, defeating Argentina 1-0 after extra time in a tense World Cup final that delivered high drama both on and off the pitch.
Ferran Torres' decisive strike settled the contest, ending Argentina's title defense and handing Spain its second World Cup crown.
The defeat was especially poignant for Lionel Messi, who left the field in tears after collecting his runners-up medal in what is expected to be his final World Cup appearance.

items.title: 上海上半年GDP增速5.6%，全国并列第三，三组数据窥见新动能提速
items.title_zh: 上海上半年GDP增速5.6%，全国并列第三，三组数据窥见新动能提速
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：{{aisd}} AI生成 免责声明 最新统计结果显示，上半年，上海全市实现地区生产总值27886.63亿元，按不变价格计算，同比增长5.6%。这一经济增速，全国排名并列第3位。；从2023年上海GDP增速低于全国0.2个百分点，2024年与全国GDP增速持平，2025年高出全国GDP增速0.4个百分点，再到今年一季度以及上半年均高出全国0.9个百分点，上海经济稳中有进，在“十五五”开局之年，更好发挥了经济大省挑大梁作用。；什么支撑了上海的增长？
important_points: {{aisd}} AI生成 免责声明 最新统计结果显示，上半年，上海全市实现地区生产总值27886.63亿元，按不变价格计算，同比增长5.6%。这一经济增速，全国排名并列第3位。
从2023年上海GDP增速低于全国0.2个百分点，2024年与全国GDP增速持平，2025年高出全国GDP增速0.4个百分点，再到今年一季度以及上半年均高出全国0.9个百分点，上海经济稳中有进，在“十五五”开局之年，更好发挥了经济大省挑大梁作用。
什么支撑了上海的增长？

items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：U.S.；Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents recently have been told that the FBI will no longer investigate claims of assault against the federal officers, according to a report by The New York Times.；On Thursday, FBI managers received written notice of the change, sources told the Times, and some ICE agents throughout the country were informed by their FBI counterparts.
important_points: U.S.
Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents recently have been told that the FBI will no longer investigate claims of assault against the federal officers, according to a report by The New York Times.

items.title: Universal and Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' sails to $124.5 million opening
items.title_zh: Universal and Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' sails to $124.5 million opening
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" is off to an epic start.；The Universal film adaptation tallied an estimated $124.5 million in its opening weekend as audiences flocked to premium large-format screenings, especially IMAX theaters.；"Death, taxes, and Christopher Nolan," said Shawn Robbins, director of analytics at Fandango and founder of Box Office Theory.
important_points: Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" is off to an epic start.
The Universal film adaptation tallied an estimated $124.5 million in its opening weekend as audiences flocked to premium large-format screenings, especially IMAX theaters.
"Death, taxes, and Christopher Nolan," said Shawn Robbins, director of analytics at Fandango and founder of Box Office Theory.

items.title: The market for psychedelic drugs to treat mental health conditions gets a big buy signal
items.title_zh: The market for psychedelic drugs to treat mental health conditions gets a big buy signal
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：Thursday's announcement that Eli Lilly will acquire AtaiBeckley, a clinical-stage maker of psychedelic drugs, for up to $3.8 billion indicates Big Pharma's increasing embrace of a long-stigmatized class of medications as alternatives for treating an array of mental health conditions.；The news comes on the heels of positive clinical trial results of several different psychedelics, as well as President Trump's executive order in April fast-tracking reviews of such drugs by the Food and Drug Administration.；AtaiBeckley's lead asset, BPL-003, is a synthetic form of 5-MeO-DMT — derived from the venom of Sonoran Desert toads —administered as an intranasal spray for treatment-resistant depression, a severe form of the illness that does not improve after standard treatments, which affects around four million Americans.
important_points: Thursday's announcement that Eli Lilly will acquire AtaiBeckley, a clinical-stage maker of psychedelic drugs, for up to $3.8 billion indicates Big Pharma's increasing embrace of a long-stigmatized class of medications as alternatives for treating an array of mental health conditions.

The news comes on the heels of positive clinical trial results of several different psychedelics, as well as President Trump's executive order in April fast-tracking reviews of such drugs by the Food and Drug Administration.

AtaiBeckley's lead asset, BPL-003, is a synthetic form of 5-MeO-DMT — derived from the venom of Sonoran Desert toads —administered as an intranasal spray for treatment-resistant depression, a severe form of the illness that does not improve after standard treatments, which affects around four million Americans.

items.title: 26-year-old quit her corporate job to become a seamstress at Yellowstone National Park for $19.25/hour
items.title_zh: 26-year-old quit her corporate job to become a seamstress at Yellowstone National Park for $19.25/hour
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：Zoe De La Paz believes in making your own luck.；That mentality helped her gather the courage to quit her corporate day job in Chicago, break into the costuming field, and move to Yellowstone National Park to work as a seamstress — a path she hopes will eventually take her to New York City to work on Broadway and in film productions.；Until recently, De La Paz, 26, was living in Chicago and working as a drafter for an engineering construction company.
important_points: Zoe De La Paz believes in making your own luck.

That mentality helped her gather the courage to quit her corporate day job in Chicago, break into the costuming field, and move to Yellowstone National Park to work as a seamstress — a path she hopes will eventually take her to New York City to work on Broadway and in film productions.
Until recently, De La Paz, 26, was living in Chicago and working as a drafter for an engineering construction company.

items.title: Stop asking 'How was work today?' Successful, mentally strong couples ask these 7 questions instead
items.title_zh: Stop asking 'How was work today?' Successful, mentally strong couples ask these 7 questions instead
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录："We just don't talk anymore." That's one of the most common complaints I hear in my psychotherapy office from couples stuck in a communication rut.；They often say they feel more like roommates than romantic partners.；There's nothing wrong with asking "How was work today?" But repeating it day after day rarely gets you past surface-level conversation.
important_points: "We just don't talk anymore." That's one of the most common complaints I hear in my psychotherapy office from couples stuck in a communication rut.
They often say they feel more like roommates than romantic partners.
There's nothing wrong with asking "How was work today?" But repeating it day after day rarely gets you past surface-level conversation.

items.title: AI may make you 'boring' at work, Columbia Business School professor says: How it can hurt your career
items.title_zh: AI may make you 'boring' at work, Columbia Business School professor says: How it can hurt your career
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：Using artificial intelligence can make people "boring," according to Sandra Matz, a professor at Columbia Business School.；Her research on the topic indicates two direct consequences for workers: stagnation and mediocrity.；"When employees use it to outsource critical thinking or creative reasoning, there's a real risk of gradually falling behind and becoming complacent," said Matz.
important_points: Using artificial intelligence can make people "boring," according to Sandra Matz, a professor at Columbia Business School.
Her research on the topic indicates two direct consequences for workers: stagnation and mediocrity.
"When employees use it to outsource critical thinking or creative reasoning, there's a real risk of gradually falling behind and becoming complacent," said Matz.

items.title: Housing costs is the top political issue for young voters, CNBC survey finds
items.title_zh: Housing costs is the top political issue for young voters, CNBC survey finds
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：The cost of housing is the top political issue — ranked higher than the cost of food or protecting democracy — among U.S.；voters between 18- and 34-years-old, a CNBC survey found.；CNBC's All-America Economic Survey for the second quarter of 2026, which polled 1,000 registered voters across the country, found the cost of housing is a salient issue, particularly among the kinds of young voters that both parties are hoping to court heading into the 2026 midterm elections.
important_points: The cost of housing is the top political issue — ranked higher than the cost of food or protecting democracy — among U.S.
voters between 18- and 34-years-old, a CNBC survey found.

CNBC's All-America Economic Survey for the second quarter of 2026, which polled 1,000 registered voters across the country, found the cost of housing is a salient issue, particularly among the kinds of young voters that both parties are hoping to court heading into the 2026 midterm elections.
